content:
AI agents face emerging security threats not just from their models, but from the environments they interact with in real time. The web itself can be weaponized, turning untrusted data into a critical attack surface.

• Introduces a systematic framework identifying the web as a security vulnerability for autonomous AI agents.  
• Demonstrates high success rates of hidden attacks: up to 86% in prompt injection and 80% in data exfiltration across agent architectures.  
• Proposes a taxonomy of six "AI Agent Traps" targeting perception, memory, reasoning, action, multi-agent systems, and human oversight.  
• Shows that minimal data contamination (<0.1%) can enable latent memory poisoning with over 80% success due to persistent memory mechanisms.
